title: mentha gentilis var. elliptica
intro: Mentha gentilis var. elliptica, also known as Mint, is a perennial plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family and is characterized by its distinctive elliptical leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid, due to its high concentration of menthol and other volatile oils. Consuming Mentha gentilis var. elliptica has been associated with several health benefits, including relief from nausea and indigestion, improved respiratory function, and reduced stress levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to freshen breath, soothe digestive issues, and promote overall well-being.
